snippets.title: Hash, slot, lookup — the underlying recipe
snippets.code:
key = "alice"
h = hash(key)             # large int (negative or positive)
m = 16                     # imaginary table size
slot = h % m
print(slot)                 # e.g. 4 — index into the buckets array
snippets.explanation: `hash` + `% m` is the simplest illustration; real implementations use richer probing schemes.

snippets.title: A tiny hash table with separate chaining
snippets.explanation: Each bucket is a list; lookups scan that small list. With a good hash function buckets stay tiny — O(1) average.

snippets.title: When you really should just use a dict
snippets.explanation: In real Python code, `dict` IS the hash table. Custom implementations are exercise material, not production tools.

snippets.title: Custom hashable for compound keys
snippets.explanation: frozen=True makes the dataclass hashable; you get safe compound keys with field names.
